response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with strong software craftsmanship and systems experience, but their profile diverges significantly from this role's core requirement of deep, hands-on C++ expertise. Their professional C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (~7 months, 2014–2015); recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js full-stack development. While their DLang low-latency, high-throughput systems work demonstrates relevant performance-engineering skills, the role's demands around industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL), MQTT/IoT protocols, and message-broker architecture at industrial scale are unevidenced.
strengths: Strong performance/systems background: low-latency async I/O services handling millions of requests per second (DLang)
Broad, sustained engineering career with TDD, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship practices matching the mentorship expectations
Event-driven architecture experience via open-source geo-data platform and microservices work
concerns: Minimal professional C++/STL experience — only ~7 months, and over a decade ago
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity, SDL, IEC 62443, or OT protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA)
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, not industrial automation or embedded platforms

key_factors.factor: Strong low-latency systems and craftsmanship skills
